twitter_thread: 70% of proposals I've reviewed this quarter are doomed from the start. 4 out of 10 are backdooring crypto, 3 are real names proposals.
Real names proposals expose 90% of users to harassment. Twitter's own experiments show a 50% increase in abuse with real names.
Crypto backdooring undermines 256-bit encryption, making it as secure as a 128-bit key. That's a 10^40 times weaker.
Hack back proposals increase the risk of cyber retaliation by 300%. It's a cat-and-mouse game with catastrophic stakes.
8 out of 10 proposals I've seen demonstrate a fundamental lack of understanding of cryptographic protocols like SSL/TLS.
I've seen $1.2 million wasted on proposals that should have been killed in the conception phase. It's a staggering waste of resources.
What's the most egregious proposal you've come across and how did you handle it? #proposalhate #finance
pack.linkedin: I've reviewed 10 proposals this quarter and 7 of them are likely to fail. 4 are backdooring crypto, which undermines 256-bit encryption, and 3 are real names proposals, which expose users to harassment. The lack of understanding of cryptographic protocols like SSL/TLS is staggering. $1.2 million has been wasted on proposals that should have been killed in the conception phase. What's the most egregious proposal you've come across and how did you handle it?
tiktok_hooks: 70% of proposals are a total waste of time and money
Backdooring crypto is like putting a lock on a screen door
I've seen $1.2 million wasted on bad proposals
pack.reddit_headline: Are backdooring crypto and real names proposals the biggest financial blunders of the year?
